rulings.Case Title: LAI PING CHAN VS. FENNY WIJAYANTI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 8. DEFENDANTS FENNY WIJAYANTI, JOHN STEWARTZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to present any competent admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact regarding the legality of the unit (and enforceability of a lease). No competent evidence presented re: NOV being issued by mistake and no authority cited for the proposition that NOV may be collaterally attacked in an Unlawful Detainer proceeding without addressing it though proper procedures. =(501/GLW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: To Compel Defenadnt To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deem Matters Admitted And For Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 9. PLAINTIFF 3884 STONERIDGE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Defenadnt To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deem Matters Admitted And For Award Of Sanctions. GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Bonnie Wilson-Nieves shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories General and Unlawful Detainer, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,185. =(501/GLW)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 10. DEFENDANT RICHARD MCWILLIAMS' Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment. GRANTED, subject to (1) Defendant posting with the Court $13,000 no later than June 25, 2018 by 4:00 p.m. and (2) thereafter filing his answer no later than 4:00 p.m. on June 25, 2018. =(501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN D. TESSLER VS. ROBERT NOLAN MIDDLETON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 11. DEFENDANT ROBERT MIDDLETON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer.
